claims.claim: The modeling target is log10(reported AEM hydroxide conductivity / mS cm⁻¹), treated as a condition-specific reported membrane property and not as a standardized intrinsic transport constant.
claims.code_example_check: validate_schema checks a positive reported_hydroxide_conductivity_ms_cm target and reports target=log10(reported_hydroxide_conductivity_ms_cm).
claims.boundary: The public example uses synthetic data and does not encode membrane conditioning, OH-exchange pretreatment, post-exchange handling before measurement, hydration or swelling state, or measurement-protocol differences.

claims.claim: Polymer identity, not conductivity row, is the primary validation unit.
claims.manuscript_location: Results 3.1-3.2; Fig. 1; Supplementary Information Table S4
claims.code_example_check: The toy run reports group_leakage.max_overlap=0 under GroupKFold.
claims.boundary: The toy run checks the public split path only; locked CatBoost metrics use the non-public observation table.

claims.claim: Row-wise random splitting is an optimistic leakage-prone control rather than the main performance estimate.
claims.manuscript_location: Abstract; Supporting Information Table S11; Supplementary Data 1 Table S11
claims.code_example_check: The toy script implements rowwise_control as a separate KFold path and reports it separately from grouped_cv.
claims.boundary: Toy row-wise numbers have no manuscript meaning; Table S11 contains the reported aggregate row-wise control values.

claims.claim: Generic molecular descriptors provide the main structure-derived descriptor gain.
claims.manuscript_location: Results 3.2; Fig. 3; Supplementary Data 1 Tables S5-S6
claims.code_example_check: The toy script evaluates nested BASE, BASE_GENERIC, and BASE_GENERIC_AEM feature sets.
claims.boundary: The toy feature-family ordering is illustrative; the reported descriptor-family conclusions are in Table S6.

claims.claim: AEM descriptors add a small repeatable increment and translate fitted-model behavior into follow-up membrane measurements.
claims.manuscript_location: Results 3.3 and 3.6; Fig. 4; Fig. 7; Fig. S7; Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6-S8 and S14
public_artifacts_to_inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6-S8 and S14
claims.code_example_check: The toy script includes AEM descriptor columns only as a public feature-family path.
claims.boundary: These fields are computed descriptors, not direct measurements of morphology, free volume, or ion pathways.

claims.claim: Predictions farther from training support carry higher risk as reported-conductivity estimates.
claims.manuscript_location: Results 3.4; Fig. 5; Fig. S6; Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6 and S13
public_artifacts_to_inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6 and S13
claims.code_example_check: The public example does not recompute manuscript support-distance bins because the selected-feature support space depends on the non-public observation table.
claims.boundary: Support-distance trends are aggregate risk indicators, not deterministic error bounds for individual new membranes.

claims.claim: Source-held-out validation shows source-level offsets, and post hoc calibration is an offset-size analysis rather than deployable performance.
claims.manuscript_location: Results 3.5; Fig. 6; Fig. S8; Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6 and S9
public_artifacts_to_inspect: Supplementary Data 1 Tables S6 and S9
claims.code_example_check: The toy schema includes a source/reporting-setting group to show the public field role without including source-derived mapping metadata.
claims.boundary: Source-derived identifiers, record counts, and full extraction tables are handled through the manuscript data-access route.

claims.claim: Exact metric regeneration requires the non-public observation table.
claims.manuscript_location: Data availability; Supplementary Code 1 README; Supplementary Data 1 Table S12
claims.boundary: The public package omits non-public literature-derived rows, internal mapping metadata, full literature-extraction tables, trained models, and row-level prediction files.
